comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (blocking=0, only a file_impact advisory, remediated via set_file_impact), signed at HEAD ea79ecca; claimed immediately. Implementation design note (key finding for the adapter): there is an IMPEDANCE MISMATCH between epic A's BatchRunner seam and sizing.py. The seam's run(req: BatchRunRequest, agent_runner: AgentStepRunner) hands a WORKFLOW AgentStepRunner (run(ctx: StepContext)->StepResult), but sizing.pass1_with_ladder(runner, cfg, plan, chunk, agentic, events) expects a rebar.llm.Runner + LLMConfig and calls passes.pass1_chunk (plan-review-specific finder). So ProductionBatchRunner cannot just call the seam's agent_runner; it must bridge. Two viable designs: (A) ProductionBatchRunner ignores the seam's agent_runner and constructs/holds its own rebar.llm.Runner + LLMConfig (from the BatchRunRequest's workflow/repo_root), driving pass1_with_ladder + shed_to_budget + checkpointing directly, and journals an enforced batch_plan (enforced:True, ladder events, shed list); OR (B) B2 injects a Runner-backed AgentStepRunner adapter and the production runner calls through it — but pass1_with_ladder's per-criterion escalation + size-ladder don't fit the one-call AgentStepRunner shape, so (A) is cleaner. sizing.py stays the logic home (no algorithm duplication); the runner is a thin orchestration adapter mapping BatchRunRequest.criteria/model_ladder/usd_budget -> chunks/cfg/cap. Equivalence tests should pin: batch packing, size-ladder escalation (batch->1-crit->model-ladder->too-big), shed-to-budget ordering (overlay->container->core), and checkpoint resume by material fingerprint. Status: claimed + planned + file_impact set, on branch feat/production-batch-runner; implementation not yet started.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Per user direction (design B1+B2 seam before implementing), wrote docs/design/batch-runner-seam.md (committed on feat/production-batch-runner). Key decisions for review: D1 keep BatchRunRequest generic + runner reconstructs PlanContext from target_ticket (replay-safe: runner not re-run on replay); D2 EXTRACT orchestrator._run_passes (Pass-1 loop) + PlanContext builder into a shared fn both orchestrator and the runner call (satisfies AC3 thin-adapter; this is the one live-gate-touching refactor); D3 runner holds its own injectable rebar.llm.Runner, seam's agent_runner unused; D4 runner computes centrality-scaled budget cap unless usd_budget overrides; D5 criterion prompt-id == registry id. Open Qs: extraction home (sizing.py vs new pass1.py), and whether to land the orchestrator refactor as its own behaviour-preserving PR first.

No cycle; orchestrator 743->491 LOC, pass1.py 313. comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
**Completion verification — PASS**

Checked each acceptance criterion against the repository:

**AC1 — ProductionBatchRunner adapts sizing.py to the BatchRunner seam, journaling an opaque batch-plan.** MET. `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` implements `ProductionBatchRunner(BatchRunner)` importing from `rebar.llm.workflow.runners` (`BatchRunner`, `BatchRunRequest`, `BatchRunResult`). It delegates all sizing/ladder/budget/checkpoint/shed logic to `pass1.run_pass1` (which internally calls `sizing.pass1_with_ladder`, `sizing.shed_to_budget`, `sizing.plan_budget_cap`, `sizing.load_checkpoint`, `sizing.save_checkpoint`). The `coverage` dict returned as `batch_plan` in `BatchRunResult.outputs` is the opaque plan the interpreter stores but never branches on.

**AC2 — Unit tests for equivalence (packing, escalation, shed-to-budget, checkpoint resume) + seam conformance.** MET. `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py` contains real (non-skipped, non-xfailed) tests covering: `test_runner_findings_equal_bespoke_run_pass1` (equivalence), `test_size_ladder_escalation_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (batch→1-crit→model-ladder→too-big, with `_BatchContextLimitRunner` and `_AllContextLimitRunner`), `test_shed_to_budget_ordering_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (overlay before core ordering), `test_checkpoint_resume_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (resume by material fingerprint), and `test_seam_conformance_and_agent_runner_unused` (opaque plan + no-branch contract).

**AC3 — sizing.py remains the single home of sizing logic; new module is a thin adapter.** MET. `production_batch_runner.py` contains no sizing/ladder/budget/shed algorithm. All computation flows through `run_pass1` → `sizing.*`. The adapter is ~186 LOC with no duplicated algorithm.

**AC4 — `make lint` + full test suite pass.** Not directly verifiable from read-only tools. The tests have no skip/xfail markers and the code imports cleanly; structurally consistent with passing. Runtime CI result cannot be confirmed from the repository state alone.

Overall verdict: **PASS** — all three verifiable acceptance criteria are clearly met by the implementation; AC4 is a CI/runtime gate outside the scope of static code verification.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

plan-review's adaptive batch orchestration — size-ladder escalation, centrality-scaled
budget cap, criteria shedding, and chunk-atomic checkpoint resume — lives in the bespoke
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` (312 LOC), OFF the workflow engine. Epic A
(`sage-votary-logic`) added a `BatchRunner` seam + a reference `DefaultBatchRunner` to the
engine. To run plan-review's Pass-1 as an engine `batch` step, the PRODUCTION batching must
plug into that seam without re-implementing the (already-correct, already-tested) sizing logic.

## What

Implement a production `BatchRunner` (the `rebar.llm.workflow.runners.BatchRunner` interface
from epic A) that adapts `sizing.py`'s public surface — `pass1_with_ladder()`,
`shed_to_budget()`, `plan_budget_cap()`, `MODEL_LADDER`, and the checkpoint load/save — to the
seam's `BatchRunRequest` → `BatchRunResult` contract. It must JOURNAL its batch-plan + fallback
as an OPAQUE plan the interpreter stores but never branches on (the epic-A invariant), and read
the recorded plan on replay. `sizing.py` stays the logic home; this story is the adapter +
wiring, not a rewrite.

## Scope

- New module (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py`) implementing the
  `BatchRunner` seam over `sizing.py`.
- Unit tests proving behavioural equivalence with the existing sizing path: batch packing,
  size-ladder escalation (batch → 1-crit → model-ladder → too-big), shed-to-budget order, and
  checkpoint resume by material fingerprint — plus seam-conformance (opaque-plan journaling, the
  interpreter-never-branches contract).
- No change to the bespoke orchestrator's behaviour yet (cutover is B5); this only makes the
  production runner available behind the seam.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A production `BatchRunner` implementation adapts `sizing.py` (ladder/budget/shed/checkpoint)
      to epic A's `BatchRunRequest`/`BatchRunResult` seam, journaling an opaque batch-plan.
- [ ] Unit tests assert behavioural equivalence with `sizing.py` for packing, size-ladder
      escalation, shed-to-budget ordering, and checkpoint resume; plus the opaque-plan/no-branch
      seam contract.
- [ ] `sizing.py` remains the single home of the sizing logic (no duplicated algorithm); the new
      module is a thin adapter.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: new ProductionBatchRunner adapting sizing.py behind epic A BatchRunner seam
reuse the adaptive batch/ladder/budget/shed/checkpoint surface (logic home unchanged)
